name: Boston Cream Pie
recipeIngredient: Flour for sifting (for Cake Layer) (1 Teaspoon)
Half and Half, hot, NOT BOILING (1 Cup)
Butter, cut in small pieces (1 Tbsp)
Semisweet Chocolate Chips (2/3 cup or 4 oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Prepare the cake pans by greasing two 8-inch round layer cake pans and lining their bottoms with parchment or waxed paper. Sprinkle flour over the paper and shake out excess, then set aside.
3. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt three times for the Cake Layer; set aside.
4. In a large bowl, beat eggs until thick and lemon-colored for 3 to 4 minutes. Gradually add sugar, beating for another 5 minutes (or hand-beat for 8 minutes).
5. Add vanilla and mix until blended.
6. Melt butter in hot milk and stir into the batter using a rubber spatula, pouring it all at once.
7. Sift flour into the mixture and fold gently, taking about 1 minute. The batter will be thin.
8. Divide batter between prepared pans and bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until a cake tester inserted in center comes out clean. Cool on wire racks for approximately 10 minutes before removing cakes from pans and continuing to cool.
9. Prepare pastry cream filling by following the steps outlined for Creme Filling below.
10. Spread the pastry cream between the cake layers, then spread chocolate glaze over the top cake layer. Allow the glaze to set for 2 hours before serving.
1. For Creme Filling:
- In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, combine sugar, flour, and salt; gradually add hot milk, stirring constantly with a wire whisk until no lumps remain.
- Cook over medium-high heat, stirring constantly until bubbly; cook for 2 minutes until thickening begins.
- Remove from heat and stir in egg yolks, beating constantly until well blended.
- Return mixture to saucepan and stir in butter, cooking 2 more minutes until thick and smooth. Add vanilla.
2. For Chocolate Glaze:
- Melt chocolate chips, milk, and butter in a small saucepan over low heat until smooth.
- Gradually combine chocolate mixture with confectioners sugar; stir in vanilla until thick enough to spread.
Yield: 8 servings
